title: Improve podman pod rm -a test
body:
When running as a user, the order of removal is database ID dependent.
This results in this test randomly failing.  This condition was
very difficult to debug and the test was missing two critical checks.
One to confirm an expected error message was produced, and another
to verify the expected running container, remains running.

Fix the container and missing error-message checks, and vastly improve
the debug-ability of this test.  Fixing the random-failures requires
intensive fixes in other areas, so that task will be left up to future
work.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: @edsantiago mind taking a peek?  This is just the useful bits extracted from debugging #2733.

@edsantiago to save you some hunting through logs, the last (PASSED) run for rootless is here:
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5299301436620800  Under the 'integration_tests' drop-down search for ``[It] podman pod rm -a doesn't remove a running container``.

It's not perfect...but IMHO a vast improvement from before where only the raw podman commands were shown.

PTAL when u have a moment.

Can't say I'm too fond of all the printfs, and I still can't wrap my head around the weird syntax, but the logic seems sound and tests pass. Nice work, it sounds like this was a doozy to figure out. /lgtm

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Thanks @edsantiago Yes the rootless issue was complex, Matt figured it out, I just poked the bees nest repeatedly with a stick.  Turns out that's easier when you can see what's going on in the nest :smile: 

I think the oddball gomega syntax perfectly complements the oddball ginkgo syntax :laughing:   Completely unnecessary but...it's what we've got to work with.
